# Content Brief Template

> Copy this file and fill in the sections. This serves as both a planning document and a structured prompt for AI content agents.

## Frontmatter

Use this YAML frontmatter at the top of your copy:

```yaml
---
brief-type: blog-post | case-study | service-page | framework-doc
target-persona: practitioner | technical-leader | honest-executive
tone-variant: warm-confident | clear-practical | thoughtful-grounded | conversational-specific
word-count: [min]-[max]
readability-target: grade-[N]
required-sections: []
seo-primary: ""
status: draft | in-review | approved | published
---
```

---

## Brief

[2-3 sentence description of what this content should accomplish and why it matters now.]

## Target Persona

[Which persona from personas.md, their JTBD statement, and what they need from this content specifically.]

## Key Messages

1. [Primary message — the one thing the reader should take away]
2. [Supporting message — evidence or context that grounds the primary]
3. [Supporting message — connection to what the reader can do next]

## Required Elements

- [ ] Specific data or evidence (no ungrounded claims)
- [ ] Real example or case reference (anonymized is fine)
- [ ] Actionable next step for the reader
- [ ] Review Checklist pass ([content-guidelines.md](../ux/content-guidelines.md#review-checklist))

## Sources / Research

[Links to prior research, data points, initiative research reports, or engagement notes to incorporate. Reference `docs/initiatives/*/research/` where applicable.]

## Voice Notes

[Any specific tone adjustments for this piece. Default tone is determined by the `tone-variant` field above. Note deviations here — e.g., "more technical than usual for this persona" or "lighter touch, this is an introductory piece."]

---

*See also: [Voice & Tone](../ux/voice-and-tone.md), [Personas](../ux/personas.md), [Messaging Framework](../ux/messaging-framework.md)*
